title: carum appuanum subsp. bulgaricum
intro: Carum appuanum subsp. bulgaricum, also known as Bulgarian carum, is a subspecies of the Carum carvi plant, commonly referred to as wild caraway or Bulgarian caraway. It is a perennial herb native to Bulgaria, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and its ability to grow in dry, rocky areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the seeds of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
